target.title: feat(qa): expose active memory toggles to scenarios
target.body:
## Summary

- Exposes active-memory session toggle helpers through the QA scenario runtime API.
- Adds the QA suite runtime implementation that writes active-memory session disable toggles into the plugin state store.

## Why

Some QA scenarios need to exercise flows where Active Memory is deliberately disabled for a specific agent session, without mutating global config or relying on test-only process state. The production Active Memory extension keys its per-session toggle records by the hashed session key, so QA Lab needs a matching helper to set and clear that same plugin-state record before running a scenario.

This keeps the behavior scenario-scoped: a scenario can disable Active Memory for the exact session under test, then later re-enable it by deleting the same session-toggle record. That makes Active Memory coverage deterministic and keeps this unrelated toggle plumbing out of the Crabline channel-driver PR.

## Validation

- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/qa-lab/src/scenario-runtime-api.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
target.author: romneyda
